vocab.word: cost an arm and a leg
vocab.meaning: rất đắt đỏ, tốn kém nhiều tiền
vocab.example: I once eyed a designer vanity mirror, but it would have cost an arm and a leg, so I settled for a more budget-friendly version.

vocab.meaning: mọi ngóc ngách, khắp mọi nơi
vocab.example: When I was moving into my new flat, I searched every nook and cranny of the local decor shops to find the perfect mirror.

vocab.meaning: dễ dàng, không gặp khó khăn gì
vocab.example: Buying a mirror online was a piece of cake, though I was terrified it might arrive shattered into pieces.

vocab.meaning: đổ sông đổ biển, lãng phí (tiền bạc/công sức)
vocab.example: I bought a cheap mirror that distorted my reflection so badly that the money I spent felt like it went straight down the drain.

vocab.meaning: một món hàng quá đắt so với giá trị thực, sự lừa đảo về giá
vocab.example: The antique mirror looked stunning, but the price the dealer quoted was a total rip-off.

vocab.meaning: tốn của ai đó một khoản tiền (thường là lớn)
vocab.example: The full-length mirror for my dressing room set me back a few hundred dollars, but it was a worthwhile investment.
Hand-crafted wooden mirrors are few and far between these days, as most people opt for mass-produced plastic frames.

vocab.meaning: rất hiếm khi, năm thì mười họa
vocab.example: I only buy mirrors once in a blue moon, usually only when I'm renovating or moving to a new house.

vocab.meaning: điều may mắn/tốt đẹp cộng thêm vào một thứ vốn đã tốt sẵn
vocab.example: The mirror I bought was already beautiful, but the fact that it came with built-in LED lighting was the icing on the cake.

vocab.meaning: vừa khít, hoàn toàn phù hợp (về kích thước hoặc vị trí)
vocab.example: I was worried about the measurements, but the mirror I bought fits the space above my fireplace like a glove.
